description_long: This is a classic stovetop moka pot, commonly used for brewing espresso-style coffee. The body is octagonal and made of metallic material, likely aluminum, with a matte silver finish. It features a prominent, angular black handle attached to the upper chamber, designed for safe gripping, and a matching black knob on the lid for easy removal. The spout is sharply defined and projects from the upper chamber, allowing for precise pouring. The base is also octagonal and slightly wider, providing stability when placed on a flat surface. There are no visible decorative patterns, giving the object a utilitarian and functional appearance.
description: A silver, octagonal stovetop moka pot with a black angular handle and knob, featuring a sharp spout and a plain, functional design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the octagonal body, black angular handle, spout, and black knob on the lid.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the full length of the black handle and the lid knob, with the spout not visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the spout clearly visible and the handle extending backward.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the spout and the geometric lines of the upper chamber, with the handle mostly hidden.
